req.title: Vital signs validation (boundary)
req.statement: An Observation with category vital-signs shall have value in clinically plausible range; out-of-range shall be flagged for review. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.
expected_outcomes: flag raised if out of range

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-044 requires that vital-signs Observations are validated against clinically plausible ranges, with out-of-range values flagged for review and a review task created. Five tests are designed: (1) EP positive – a normal in-range heart-rate value is accepted and stored without a flag; (2) NEG negative – a clearly impossible value (negative heart rate) is rejected or flagged with a review task; (3) BVA boundary – heart rate at the exact minimum accepted boundary (30 bpm) is stored without flagging; (4) BVA boundary – heart rate one unit below the minimum boundary (29 bpm) triggers a review flag; (5) DT – a combination of an out-of-range value AND a missing effectiveDateTime tests the decision table intersection of two validation rules. Each test calls a shared <clinician_auth_headers> fixture and a <build_observation> helper to keep payloads concise and deterministic.

tests.name: test_vital_signs_inrange_heart_rate_stored_without_flag
preconditions: No prior Observation with the same identifier exists
actions: Build an Observation payload with category=vital-signs, code=heart-rate, valueQuantity.value=72 (bpm), valid effectiveDateTime
POST payload to /Observation with clinician auth headers
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body does NOT contain a 'flag' or 'reviewTask' element
trace: EP: valid partition for heart-rate (30–250 bpm)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_vital_signs_inrange_heart_rate_stored_without_flag(
    clinician_auth_headers,  # <fixture>
    fhir_base_url,           # <fixture>
    build_observation,       # <helper_fn>
    unique_identifier,       # <helper_fn>
):
    payload = build_observation(
        identifier=unique_identifier(),
        category="vital-signs",
        code="8867-4",
        value=72,
        unit="/min",
        effective="2024-01-15T10:00:00Z",
        status="final",
    )
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" in body
    assert "reviewTask" not in body
    assert body.get("status") == "final"

tests.name: test_vital_signs_impossible_heart_rate_triggers_review_task
preconditions: No prior Observation with the same identifier exists
actions: Build an Observation payload with category=vital-signs, code=heart-rate, valueQuantity.value=-5 (impossible negative bpm), valid effectiveDateTime
POST payload to /Observation with clinician auth headers
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: If 201: response body contains a 'reviewTask' or 'flag' element indicating out-of-range
If 422: response body contains an OperationOutcome with severity='error' referencing range violation
trace: R-HLT-044 – error pathway: review task created
NEG: value outside any plausible clinical range
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_vital_signs_impossible_heart_rate_triggers_review_task(
    clinician_auth_headers,
    fhir_base_url,
    build_observation,
    unique_identifier,
):
    payload = build_observation(
        identifier=unique_identifier(),
        category="vital-signs",
        code="8867-4",
        value=-5,
        unit="/min",
        effective="2024-01-15T10:00:00Z",
        status="final",
    )
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (201, 422), f"Unexpected status {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    if resp.status_code == 201:
        assert "reviewTask" in body or body.get("flag") is not None, (
            "Out-of-range value must produce a reviewTask or flag"
        )
    else:
        issues = body.get("issue", [])
        severities = [i.get("severity") for i in issues]
        assert "error" in severities

tests.name: test_vital_signs_heart_rate_at_minimum_boundary_accepted
preconditions: Minimum accepted heart rate boundary is 30 bpm per clinical rules
No prior Observation with the same identifier exists
actions: Build an Observation payload with category=vital-signs, code=heart-rate, valueQuantity.value=30 (exact lower boundary), valid effectiveDateTime
POST payload to /Observation with clinician auth headers
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: No 'reviewTask' or 'flag' element in response body
trace: R-HLT-044 – boundary case: lower inclusive boundary
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

LOWER_BOUNDARY_HR = 30

def test_vital_signs_heart_rate_at_minimum_boundary_accepted(
    clinician_auth_headers,
    fhir_base_url,
    build_observation,
    unique_identifier,
):
    payload = build_observation(
        identifier=unique_identifier(),
        category="vital-signs",
        code="8867-4",
        value=LOWER_BOUNDARY_HR,
        unit="/min",
        effective="2024-01-15T10:00:00Z",
        status="final",
    )
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" in body
    assert "reviewTask" not in body
    stored_value = body["valueQuantity"]["value"]
    assert stored_value == LOWER_BOUNDARY_HR

tests.name: test_vital_signs_heart_rate_one_below_minimum_boundary_flagged
preconditions: Minimum accepted heart rate boundary is 30 bpm per clinical rules
No prior Observation with the same identifier exists
actions: Build an Observation payload with category=vital-signs, code=heart-rate, valueQuantity.value=29 (one unit below lower boundary), valid effectiveDateTime
POST payload to /Observation with clinician auth headers
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: If 201: 'reviewTask' or 'flag' element present in response
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BELOW_LOWER_BOUNDARY_HR = 29

def test_vital_signs_heart_rate_one_below_minimum_boundary_flagged(
    clinician_auth_headers,
    fhir_base_url,
    build_observation,
    unique_identifier,
):
    payload = build_observation(
        identifier=unique_identifier(),
        category="vital-signs",
        code="8867-4",
        value=BELOW_LOWER_BOUNDARY_HR,
        unit="/min",
        effective="2024-01-15T10:00:00Z",
        status="final",
    )
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (201, 422), f"Unexpected status {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    if resp.status_code == 201:
        assert "reviewTask" in body or body.get("flag") is not None
    else:
        assert any(i.get("severity") == "error" for i in body.get("issue", []))

tests.name: test_vital_signs_out_of_range_value_with_missing_timestamp_decision_table
preconditions: effectiveDateTime is a mandatory element per FHIR vital-signs profile
No prior Observation with the same identifier exists
actions: Build an Observation payload with category=vital-signs, code=heart-rate, valueQuantity.value=999 (out-of-range), effectiveDateTime OMITTED
POST payload to /Observation with clinician auth headers
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response OperationOutcome contains at least two issues: one for missing effectiveDateTime and one for out-of-range value
OR a single composite issue citing both violations
No 201 response accepted for this combination
trace: R-HLT-044 – error pathway: multiple simultaneous validation failures
DT: column (out-of-range=TRUE, missing-timestamp=TRUE) => reject with compound OperationOutcome
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_vital_signs_out_of_range_value_with_missing_timestamp_decision_table(
    clinician_auth_headers,
    fhir_base_url,
    build_observation,
    unique_identifier,
):
    payload = build_observation(
        identifier=unique_identifier(),
        category="vital-signs",
        code="8867-4",
        value=999,
        unit="/min",
        effective=None,  # omit timestamp
        status="final",
    )
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, (
        f"Expected 422 for compound violation, got {resp.status_code}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert len(issues) >= 1, "OperationOutcome must contain at least one issue"
    diagnostics = " ".join(i.get("diagnostics", "") for i in issues)
    has_range_error = any("range" in i.get("diagnostics", "").lower() for i in issues)
    has_ts_error = any(
        "effective" in i.get("diagnostics", "").lower() or
        "timestamp" in i.get("diagnostics", "").lower()
        for i in issues
    )
    assert has_range_error or has_ts_error, (
        f"Expected range or timestamp error in issues; got: {diagnostics}"
    )
